**lebop v0.0.2** — polish + correctness pass.

This release closes ~30 bugs and rough edges surfaced by multi-agent
review of v0.0.1. The dominant theme: **the error envelope contract now
holds across every MCP tool surface**, including SDK-layer input validation
and silent-drop edge cases that previously masked data-loss.

## Highlights

- **MCP `update_issue` no longer silently drops state/assignee/labels**
  when called without an explicit `team` arg. Team is now auto-derived
  from the TEAM-NN identifier prefix (same pattern as `bulk_update_issues`).
  Pre-fix the response reported `isError: false` but the field never
  actually changed — `get_issue` would confirm the lie.
- **MCP zod input-validation errors now emit the structured envelope.**
  Pre-fix every wrong-argument call returned a prose string
  (`"MCP error -32602: ..."`) instead of `{schema_version, error: {code:
  "invalid_arguments", message, hint, issues: [...]}}`. Clients can now
  `switch (error.code)` over the full taxonomy without grepping prose.
- **Archive-bug surface complete.** `get_initiative`, `get_milestone`, and
  `list_initiative_updates` use the list-shape query with `includeArchived:
  true` (the single-record `*(id:)` getters silently drop archived rows
  for these entity types — Linear-side bug). `archived_at` is now
  surfaced on every milestone shape.
- **`tryMapToNull`** — a single helper standardizes the "missing → null"
  SDK-boundary contract across 8 `get_*` lib functions: `get_project`,
  `get_initiative`, `get_milestone`, `get_document`, `get_cycle`,
  `get_agent_session`, `get_team`, `list_workflow_states`. Calls with a
  bad UUID return `{<entity>: null}` instead of a raw "Entity not found".
- **Delete idempotency is uniform.** `comment delete`, `document delete`,
  `label delete`, `initiative delete`, `milestone delete`, `project
  delete` all return `{status: "deleted" | "already-absent"}` and exit 0
  when re-run on an already-deleted entity. Pre-fix the convention split
  3 ways across commands.

## Breaking changes (MCP surface)

These arg renames are wire-breaking for any client that hard-coded
the old name. All are consistency tightenings we don't expect to
revisit. Old names return `code: invalid_arguments`.

- **`list_team_members`**: arg `team_key` → `team`. Aligns with every
  other tool that takes a team identifier (`list_issues`, `list_projects`,
  etc.).
- **`set_workspace_default_team`**: arg `team_key` → `team`. Same
  rationale. Response envelope key also renamed.
- **`delete_comment`**: arg `comment_id` → `id`. Response envelope
  field `comment_id` → `id` (matches every other delete tool —
  `delete_project`, `delete_milestone`, etc.).
- **`update_comment`**: arg `comment_id` → `id`. Same rationale.
- **`get_team`**: arg `key_or_id` → `id`. The name's accepts-key-or-uuid
  semantics moved into the schema description (`"Team key OR UUID"`).
- **`get_initiative`**: arg `id_or_name` → `id`. The accepts-UUID-or-name
  semantics moved into the schema description and aligns with the
  rest of the initiative family (`update_initiative`,
  `archive_initiative`, `unarchive_initiative`) which already used `id`.

These error-shape changes are wire-breaking for any client that
parsed the prose payload:

- **Zod validation errors** now return `{schema_version, error: {code:
  "invalid_arguments", message, hint, issues: [...]}}` instead of
  `"MCP error -32602: Input validation error: ..."` as a single text
  blob.
- **Unrecognized argument keys** now also return the structured envelope
  with `error.code: "invalid_arguments"` and `issues: [{code:
  "unrecognized_keys", keys: [...], path: [], message}]`. Pre-fix `update_issue
  identifier:"ENG-1" labl:"x"` (typo'd plural) silently dropped the key
  and returned "nothing to update".
- **`lebop diff <id> --json`** now exits 1 when drift exists (matches
  the human-mode behavior, mirrors `git diff --exit-code`). CI gates
  piping `lebop diff --json | jq ...` previously silently passed.
- **MCP `get_issue` returns `{issue: null}` on miss** (was: error
  envelope with `code: "validation_error"`). Aligns with the 7 other
  `get_*` MCP tools' not-found contract. CLI commands
  (`lebop show`, `lebop pull`) are unaffected — they continue printing
  the `not found: <id>` error to stderr with exit 1.
- **`lebop attachment delete <id>`** now requires `--yes` (parity with
  every other destructive delete). Scripts calling it without `--yes`
  will exit 1 with a confirmation hint.
- **CLI `--json` errors now emit a structured envelope on stdout**
  instead of chalk prose on stderr when the failing subcommand has
  `--json`. Shape: `{ok: false, schema_version: 1, error: {code,
  message, hint}}`. Human mode (no `--json`) still emits stderr prose.

## Fixes

### Correctness

- `update_issue` (MCP) auto-derives team from TEAM-NN identifier; throws
  `ValidationError` when name-shaped fields are passed but team can't be
  resolved. The pre-fix silent-skip is gone.
- `update_issue` parent resolution no longer gated on team (it's
  workspace-wide via `resolveIssueIdByIdentifier`).
- `lebop diff --json` honors `git diff --exit-code` semantics — exit
  code set BEFORE the JSON branch returns.
- `lebop lint <path...>` with all-missing paths exits 1 with a clear
  error (was silently "0 file(s) checked").
- `findLink` defensive `.toUpperCase()` on both forward + inverse
  branches — guards against future call sites that skip identifier
  normalization or Linear alias-resolution surfacing lowercase
  identifiers.
- `diff_issue` (MCP) tolerates team renames — looks up canonical
  identifier via Linear first, then uses it for cache lookup.

### Error envelope completeness

- New `InvalidArgumentsError` class + `code: "invalid_arguments"` for
  schema-level rejections. Distinct from `validation_error` (lib-level
  business-rule failure).
- `ResolveError` now emits `code: "validation_error"` (was the
  undocumented `code: "resolve_error"`).
- `raw_graphql` syntax errors map to `validation_error` (was `unknown`).
- `list_issues` / `list_labels` now error with `code: "not_found"` when
  `team` arg doesn't match any team. Pre-fix `list_issues` silently
  filtered to empty and `list_labels` silently returned workspace-wide
  labels.
- `cache_status` error hint no longer says "pass --team KEY" in MCP
  responses (the MCP equivalent is the `team` arg).

### CLI polish

- `lebop set <field> <id> <value>` auto-derives team from `<id>` —
  `--team` is no longer required when the identifier is TEAM-NN.
- `lebop list --priority N` validates 0..4 at the boundary; out-of-range
  / non-numeric values exit 1 with a clear error (was silently "no
  matching issues").
- `lebop link <issue> <url>` is now idempotent — re-running with the
  same URL returns the existing attachment with `status: "already-linked"`.
- `lebop set links` now detects flag-shaped tokens (`--team`, `--json`,
  etc.) and emits a tailored hint pointing at the `--` argument-list
  sentinel.
- `lebop milestone create` and `lebop document create` accept
  `--project-id <uuid>` in addition to `--project <name-or-id>`. Parity
  with `lebop new`.
- `NO_COLOR=1` now wins over `FORCE_COLOR=1` per the no-color.org spec.
- All 6 destructive-delete commands replaced `process.exit(1)` with
  `process.exitCode = 1; return` (uniform with the rest of the codebase;
  testable via in-process spawning).

### MCP polish

- `update_initiative` accepts name OR UUID via `resolveInitiativeId`,
  matching `get_initiative` and `archive_initiative`.

## Coverage additions

~37 new tests in this release:

- `tryMapToNull` direct unit coverage (7 cases — success / NotFoundError /
  raw SDK Entity-not-found / RateLimitError / AuthError / ValidationError /
  unclassified-error passthrough).
- `findLink` case-folding coverage (6 cases — hardening guard).
- `lebop diff` command — 4 integration tests (matching cache, drifted
  cache, human + `--json` for each).
- `lebop lint --strict` exit-code regression lock (2 tests).
- `updateIssue` team/name-field fix — 11 tests covering name-field
  derive, malformed identifier rejection, parent-without-team,
  explicit-team override, priority-only, `@me`/`me` viewer resolution.
- MCP H11 envelope — 5 tests covering required-field rejection, range
  rejection, unknown tool, success-path delegation, lib-error passthrough.
- HIGH-4 `parseLinkToken` flag-shape detection (3 tests).
- A26 `auth token` masking (2 tests).
- Plus assorted shape additions for `archived_at` propagation and
  envelope tests.

Plus additions for unrecognized-keys envelopes, top-level projects parity,
soft-delete pre-flight, etc.

Total: **559 tests across 42 test files** (up from 472).

## Install

```sh
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/N0xMare/lebop/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```

macOS / Linux, x64 / arm64. Verifies SHA256 against the `SHA256SUMS`
attached to this release. Self-contained binary at
`~/.local/bin/lebop`. See the
[README](https://github.com/N0xMare/lebop#readme) for source-install
and `LEBOP_INSTALL_DIR` / `LEBOP_VERSION` overrides.

## Upgrade notes

If you're scripting against the MCP server:

1. **Replace `team_key:` with `team:`** for `list_team_members` and
   `set_workspace_default_team`.
2. **Replace `comment_id:` with `id:`** for `delete_comment` and
   `update_comment`. The `delete_comment` response envelope field also
   renames to `id`.
3. **Replace `key_or_id:` with `id:`** for `get_team`.
4. **Replace `id_or_name:` with `id:`** for `get_initiative`.
5. **Update error handling** if you were parsing
   `"MCP error -32602: ..."` strings — the new shape is
   `{schema_version, error: {code: "invalid_arguments", ...}}`.
6. **Update CI gates** that pipe `lebop diff --json` — it now exits 1
   on drift. Add `|| true` if you want the previous lenient behavior.

If you're calling the CLI: no changes needed (auto-derive + idempotency
fixes are strictly safer than pre-fix behavior).

[`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/N0xMare/lebop/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
